channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kahama Market Fire Causes Significant Losses for Traders
item.description: A fierce morning fire on July 15, 2026, gutted 32 market stalls in Kahama's Dodoma Industrial Area, Shinyanga Region, causing heavy losses to 36 timber traders. The blaze, which started at 6 am, sprea...

item.title: Dogs intervene to protect women from alleged attacker at Kooyong Oval
item.description: A dramatic incident unfolded at Kooyong Oval in Victoria, where a pack of dogs reportedly intervened to prevent an alleged attacker from harming two women. Witnesses described the chaotic scene, with ...

item.title: Blue singer Lee Ryan convicted of racially aggravated assault on air steward
item.description: Lee Ryan, former member of the boyband Blue, is set to face sentencing for a racially aggravated assault on an air steward following the rejection of his appeal. The incident occurred during a flight ...

item.title: Argentina Advances to World Cup Final After Dramatic 2-1 Win Over England
item.description: In a thrilling semi-final clash at the World Cup, reigning champions Argentina staged a remarkable comeback to defeat England 2-1 and secure their place in the final. Trailing 1-0 heading into the fin...

item.title: Eight South African Golfers Aim for Claret Jug at Royal Birkdale
item.description: Eight South African golfers, including 2023 South African Open champion Casey Jarvis and former major contender Hennie du Plessis, will compete in The Open Championship at Royal Birkdale. Du Plessis, ...

item.title: US Conducts Strikes on Iran Amid Escalating Conflict
item.description: The United States has reportedly launched a series of military strikes against Iran, marking a significant escalation in the ongoing regional conflict. According to sources in Tehran, Washington, and ...

item.title: Ontario School Board Launches Bullying Review Following Student Death
item.description: The Limestone District School Board in Ontario is conducting an independent review of school safety following the tragic death of a student at Bath Public School earlier this year. The review, initiat...

item.title: Helena Citrónová: A Holocaust Survivor's Unlikely Relationship with an SS Officer
item.description: Helena Citrónová was a young Slovak Jewish woman born in 1922 who endured unimaginable horrors during World War II. In March 1942, she was forcibly transported to Auschwitz as part of the first mass d...

item.title: Plant-based diets linked to lower heart disease risk in new research
item.description: A recent study published in the British Medical Journal has found that individuals following a plant-based diet are significantly less likely to develop heart disease compared to those consuming a die...

item.title: Gautam Adani's legal battle: Court probes DOJ's decision to drop charges
item.description: Indian billionaire Gautam Adani has submitted a sworn affidavit stating he was unaware of any quid pro quo offer from the US Department of Justice (DOJ) that may have influenced the dismissal of charg...

item.title: Court Martial Suspended Over Health Crisis Of Detained Officer In Illegal DIA Detention Facility

item.title: Deworming Program Continues Amid Safety Concerns
item.description: The Department of Health (DOH) and Department of Education (DepEd) continue their nationwide deworming program in public schools despite a recent incident where an 8-year-old student died after consum...

item.title: France and Tanzania Strengthen Bilateral Ties Through Cultural and Economic Collaboration
item.description: France marked Bastille Day in Tanzania with a vibrant 'Afro Bastille' event in Dar es Salaam, highlighting cultural ties and reinforcing diplomatic-economic partnerships. The celebration featured art,...

item.title: Supreme Court grants Odisha time to decide on Dara Singh's remission plea by August 19
item.description: The Supreme Court has granted the Odisha government until August 19 to decide on the remission plea of Dara Singh, a Bajrang Dal member serving a life sentence for the 1999 murder of Australian missio...

item.title: Delta State Police Arrest Four Suspects Linked to Armed Robbery and Cult Activities
item.description: The police in Delta State have arrested four suspects linked to armed robbery and cultism in separate operations across the state. A joint team of police operatives and members of the Civilian Anti-Cu...

item.title: Calls for Data Centre Pause Amid AI Policy Announcements
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese's recent speech on artificial intelligence has sparked calls for a temporary halt to data centre approvals and construction. The Labor government's announcement of new ...

item.title: U.S. Conducts Daytime Airstrikes Against Iran, Targeting Coastal Defenses and Missile Sites
item.description: The U.S. military executed renewed airstrikes against Iranian regime targets in the Persian Gulf, marking the fourth consecutive day of operations. Central Command (CENTCOM) confirmed the strikes last...

item.title: Speeches on SEA Games Probe Overshadow Oral Arguments in Latest News
item.description: The news article discusses how speeches criticizing the probe into the Southeast Asian (SEA) Games scandal nearly overshadowed the formal oral arguments during a recent legal proceeding. The original ...

item.title: March and March Movement strengthens anti-illegal migration alliances and addresses internal challenges
item.description: The March and March Movement, a group focused on combating illegal migration and crime, has pledged to consolidate alliances with supportive organizations following a meeting in Durban. The gathering,...

item.title: Dermot Murnaghan Passes Away After Cancer Battle, Leaves Legacy in British Journalism
item.description: Dermot Murnaghan, a respected British news presenter, died of cancer at 68 after a four-decade career across BBC, ITV, Channel 4, and Sky News. His death marks the end of an era in British broadcastin...
